sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.54 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Is it true that in the group of coloured braids only the identity braid is a~conjugate to its inverse? (For definition see Archive, 10.24.) { G.\,S.\,Makanin} { 11.55.} Is it true that extraction of roots in the group of coloured braids is uniquely determined? { G.\,S.\,Makanin} The answers to both 11.54 and 11.55 are affirmative, since the group$K_n$of coloured braids is embeddable into the group...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
